description_long: This object is a roughly triangular, knapped stone with a pointed tip and broad base, resembling a primitive tool or arrowhead. Its surface is uneven, with visible flake scars and chipped edges, suggesting it was shaped by deliberate striking. The stone has a matte, slightly mottled appearance, with subtle variations in gray and brown tones. The profile is thin and sharp-edged, with one side showing more pronounced ridges and indentations from the knapping process. The overall form is asymmetrical, with one edge more curved and the other straighter, giving it a natural, hand-crafted look. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial markings.
description: A pointed, knapped stone with a triangular shape, rough edges, and a matte gray-brown surface. It has visible flake scars and an asymmetrical, hand-crafted appearance.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and triangular, with visible flake scars and a pointed tip at the top.
description_view_1: A thin side profile reveals the sharpness and narrowness of the stone, emphasizing its blade-like edge.
description_view_2: The flat face of the stone is shown, highlighting its asymmetrical outline and smooth, matte surface.
description_view_3: Another thin side profile, similar to view 1, showing the stone's narrowness and chipped edge details.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or loose obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular flake scars and mottling, with no regular or artificial patterns; natural stone texture dominates.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and matte, with minor signs of weathering but no heavy erosion or lichen growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or grassy urban surfaces, may partially blend with natural landscaping stones or gravel.
